strengths: Novel application of truth-maintenance systems to agent memory
Clear problem framing: moving from similarity to truth-consistent retrieval
Strong empirical results on knowledge-update tasks (100% accuracy)
Significant efficiency gains over baseline (55% token reduction)
Well-designed ablation isolating the linker's contribution
weaknesses: Temporal-reasoning results are weak (40%) and sample size is tiny (N=5)
Overall accuracy only matches the flat baseline, does not exceed it
Lack of statistical significance reporting (e.g., error bars, p-values)
No comparison to other temporal or belief-revision baselines
Limited evaluation on the full LongMemEval benchmark (only -S subset)
required_revisions: Replace placeholder temporal-reasoning sample size (N=5) with full evaluation across all seeds and report standard deviation.
Clarify the exact size and composition of LongMemEval-S used (500 questions total, but slice breakdowns unclear).
Provide statistical significance tests for the reported accuracy differences.
Include a comparison to a simple temporal filtering baseline (e.g., recency-based retrieval) to better isolate the contribution of the graph structure.
Justify the choice of hyperparameters (e.g., similarity thresholds) and show sensitivity if possible.
